title: Serial Free-Trial Harvester with Email Domain Rotation Pattern
narrative: Fraudster systematically exploits 7-day or 30-day free trials across multiple subscription services using rotating temporary email domains. Actor creates accounts with 8-15 different services within 48 hours, always canceling 12-24 hours before trial expiration. Pattern shows consistent card instrument (card1) paired with sequential email variations across disposable domains (guerrillamail.com, temp-mail.org, 10minutemail.net). TransactionAmt always $0.00 for authorization holds, but card1_txn_count_7d reaches 12-18 distinct merchants. Each signup occurs during late-night hours (txn_hour 23-04) suggesting automated scripting. ProductCD consistently shows 'W' (online services). P_emaildomain cycles through 5+ temporary providers while billing addr1 remains constant. The velocity of trial signups (6+ per day) combined with zero actual paid conversions flags systematic abuse rather than legitimate shopping behavior.
indicators: card1_txn_count_7d between 12-18 with all TransactionAmt = $0.00 authorization holds
P_emaildomain shows 5+ distinct temporary/disposable email providers within 48-hour window
100% of transactions occur during txn_hour 23-04 (late night/early morning)
Cancellation requests submitted 12-24 hours before each trial expiration across all merchants
addr1 and dist1 remain constant while P_emaildomain rotates on every transaction
ProductCD = 'W' across all transactions with 8-15 unique merchant IDs (MIDs)
Zero conversions to paid subscriptions despite 12+ trial activations in 7-day period
summary: A single payment card generates 12-18 authorization holds across different merchants within 7 days, all at $0.00, using 5+ rotating disposable email domains while keeping the same billing address. All activity occurs between 11 PM and 4 AM, and every trial is canceled just before expiration with zero paid conversions.
reasoning: The actor is systematically extracting free value from multiple services without ever intending to pay, using automation and temporary emails to circumvent trial limits. The late-night clustering, email rotation with static address, and perfect cancellation timing reveal deliberate abuse rather than a customer exploring legitimate options.
indicator_explanations: Card is used repeatedly for zero-dollar trial authorizations with no real purchases
Email address cycles through multiple disposable providers in rapid succession
Activity happens exclusively in overnight hours when fraud teams are offline
Trials are systematically canceled right before billing would actually occur
Physical location stays fixed while email identity changes with each signup
Same product type purchased from many different merchants in short timeframe
Not a single trial converts to paying subscription despite heavy usage
